2604.11663.title: Why Do Large Language Models Generate Harmful Content?
2604.11663.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been shown to generate harmful content. However, the underlying causes of such behavior remain under explored. We propose a causal mediation analysis-based approach to identify the causal factors responsible for harmful generation. Our method performs a multi-granular analysis across model layers, modules (MLP and attention blocks), and individual neurons. Extensive experiments on state-of-the-art LLMs indicate that harmful generation arises in the later layers of the model, results primarily from failures in MLP blocks rather than attention blocks, and is associated with neurons that act as a gating mechanism for harmful generation. The results indicate that the early layers in the model are used for a contextual understanding of harmfulness in a prompt, which is then propagated through the model, to generate harmfulness in the late layers, as well as a signal indicating harmfulness through MLP blocks. This is then further propagated to the last layer of the model, specifically to a sparse set of neurons, which receives the signal and determines the generation of harmful content accordingly.
2604.11663.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）已被證明會生成有害內容。<lb/>然而，這種行為的根本原因仍然未被充分探討。<lb/>我們提出了一種基於因果中介分析的方法，以識別負責有害生成的因果因素。<lb/>我們的方法在模型層、模塊（MLP和注意力區塊）以及個別神經元之間進行多層次分析。<lb/>對於最先進的LLMs進行的廣泛實驗表明，有害生成主要發生在模型的後期層，主要源於MLP區塊的失敗，而非注意力區塊，並且與作為有害生成閘控機制的神經元相關。<lb/>結果表明，模型的早期層用於對提示中有害性的上下文理解，然後這一理解通過模型傳播，以在後期層生成有害性，以及通過MLP區塊發出指示有害性的信號。<lb/>這一信號進一步傳播到模型的最後一層，特別是到一組稀疏的神經元，這些神經元接收信號並相應地決定生成有害內容。
2604.11663.author: Rajesh Ganguli et.al.
